existing_rules_covering.explanation: This is exactly the mirrored-runtime failure COD-004 was written for: the live board kept its own reviewer/leader parsing path instead of importing the shared review-engine implementation, so fixes to the shared parser would not reach the active runtime.

The duplicated `preValidateResponse` and `cleanJsonResponse` helpers are a direct example of diverging local copies of shared governed runtime logic. COD-004 already requires one canonical implementation and live consumers importing it.

The `pathExists` drift is another copy-divergence problem: two live consumers maintain separate implementations with different behavior and cost. COD-004 covers consolidating shared runtime helpers so consumers do not keep divergent local versions.

The issue is a silent broad catch that hides the real loader failure. IMP-007 already requires every catch to log or propagate instead of swallowing errors, which would prevent `import()` failures from being masked as 'not found'.
